feed.title: Lizstar’s Trashcan
feed.subtitle: A place where Lizstar writes about the stupid junk she wants to write about. Special interests include yuri, video games, writing, yuri, obscure video games, and a lot of yuri.

entry.title: LizstarTrek - Lower Decks Season 1
entry.content:
Every Sunday my Twitch subs and I watch Star Trek in my Discord. Here are my reviews and thoughts on each of the episodes as I see it. If you’re curious about a nerd’s views on Star Trek episodes, please read on, I go quite in depth with some of these episodes! I’ve watched some Star Trek before but not all of it!

I know very little about this show except it was made by nerds like me with a very different sense of humor. God, please be good.

Second Contact - ENT 3.5/10

We’re introduced very quickly to two Ensigns. Boimler and Mariner. Boimler is high strung, lame, and has illusions of grandeur, making a fake captains log. Mariner is a kinda shitty killjoy who has EXTREME illusions of grandeur. Mariner injurs Boimler with a batleth while drunk on Romulan Ale. Okay, next scene!

We’re now introduced to Ensign Tendi, an Orion who’s very bright eyed and bushy tailed, but y’know, she’s lower decks so she’ll be getting beat down over time I’m sure. They also meet another ensign, Rutherford, an engineer who’s a cyborg and ALSO a nervous wreck. There’s a lot of that here. Hey, is this review fast and hard to follow without a lot of detail? Yes! That’s HOW THIS SHOW IS INTRODUCED.

Everyone is talking so fucking fast, jokes are flung in your face every 5 seconds with no time to breath and then we move right on to the next joke or the next scene. If you speak REALLY fast everyone will think what you say is smart, except for Funny this time. It is… NOT easy to follow. And the jokes are pretty rough, IIRC. Lots of awkward humor, which is VERY not my speed.

Boimler is told by her captain to keep an eye on Mariner, and he takes it way WAY too seriously and makes things really fucking weird. That’s his character trait! The two of them scare off some aliens at the planet they’re on and then get attacked by a spider that shoots webbing out of its butt. You might think that’s weird for me to specify, but the show sure does! Ha ha, butts are funny!

Meanwhile, one of the bridge officers got infected with a zombie virus and its spreading violently through the ship. The show cuts from scenes of graphic zombie violence to Rutherford having a date. It’s incongruent. That’s the point and it’s supposed to be funny…. okay, it’s actually not bad. I kinda like this part. They’re walking around “on a date walk” as they blast zombies and talk about the music they’re into. Thats kinda cute. Unfortunately the date doesn’t work cause Rutherford is kind of a huge dork.

Why are their clothes off? Don’t worry about it.

Boimler gets covered in alien slime goo and is taken to sick bay cause it’s a cure for the virus. All of the bridge crew ignore Boimler and his contributions and they are basically having their own Star Trek episode while the Lower Deckers are in the background. I think that’s supposed to be the entire premise and conceit of the entire show but also I kinda hate it? It’s like, really unpleasant to watch.

You gotta take this show with a BIT more of a grain of salt cause it’s a hyperbolic joke show, but this is still not my favorite general conceit. And I’d LOVE a show about lower decks people.

Anyways, Boimler does NOT narc on Mariner, and they become “friends”. Mariner is also the captain’s daughter, who hates her and wants to throw her in the brig for no reason.

This was so fucking hard to follow and write a review of holy HELL. This was a very rough introduction, but I can see how there are sparks of something good here.

Envoy - ENT 4.5/10

Our episode starts with Mariner harassing some evil space wisp with super cosmic power. She bullies it into using up its power and it dies slamming into the captain. Ha ha?

Anyways this episode has an A and a B plot. The A plot is that Boimler is escorting a Klingon warrior down to a planet, while Mariner forces her way on board and ruins it cause she’s actually besties with the Klingon.

Meanwhile, Rutherford promised Tendi that he’s do a thing with her once but he keeps overloading himself on Engineering work. So he decides to change his entire career and is kinda bummed about it. Doesn’t do anything half assed, does he?

Boimler and Mariner get themselves stranded in a seedy Klingon section of a planet cause the Klingon stole their shuttle for a joke.

Rutherford is trying command simulations with the first officer, Jack Ransom. He blows up the ship and kills all the children, specifically. Jack is WEIRDLY into the idea of killing children. Is that. Supposed to be a joke? What? What is this character?

The bridge officers are, for the most part, kind of assholes. The captain and first officer especially, but there are two I kinda like so far. The doctor is a ratty mean Caitian who, while blunt, actually seems to care and understand how people work? And the security officer, who’s a giant braga braga Bajoran and seems like fun tbh. He even takes Rutherford wanting to go back to Engineering super well cause he’s just a nice guy! That’s cool.

Mariner, despite not giving a shit about anything, is instantly good at every single thing she does. It makes Boimler feel so bad that he decides to quit. Then he gets his groove back after being racist at a Ferengi saying they are the least trustworthy race in space. What? What the fuck? Quark did not die for your sins so that you could talk like that. Not to mention ROM. …Okay, the whole Ferengi thing was just a plot by Mariner to make Boimler feel better. Good on her. Bad on you, Boimler >:(

A little better.

Temporal Edict - ENT 5/10

The captain has a bug up her ass and finds out that the lower decks crew have a thing called “Buffer Time” where they do things slightly half assed by giving extra time estimates on things so they look good and don’t have to rush. She puts a tighter leash on everyone. This IMMEDIATELY backfires and everyone is super overworked. Ah yes, let’s reinvent capitalistic practices. Everyone hates it. Oh except Boimler, he’s having a good time.

Everyone being overworked and exhausted means they make mistakes and piss off some aliens they were supposed to give a gift to.

I think Captain Freeman might be my least favorite Captain so far, and that’s nuts considering one of the options is Captain Lorca.

Ransom and Mariner fight over who has to fight in a battle to the death. Ransom stabs her to get her to stop. There’s also some kind of sexual tension between the two of them I am REALLY not into.

Ha ha ethics are for losers and pencil pushers. I won’t justify that because the show doesn’t.

Anyways they win by loosening up, of course. Boimler suggests it and gets something named after him, “The Boimler Effect”, where you’re supposed to cut corners and do what you can to get out of more work. He is not happy. And far in the future, Boimler is revered for being a lazy piece of shit. But he’s not as revered as “the most important person in Starfleet History”

This is correct.

This one had two jokes I liked!!

Moist Vessel - ENT 5/10

Mariner is rude during a meeting with another captain and this pisses the captain off. She does it cause she’s yawning cause “it’s too much information to take in all at once”. I dunno if this show has the right to say that, considering how fucking fast paced it is.

Why are there class divided replicators? That’s weird.

The captain assigns Mariner to the shittiest jobs on the ship to try and force her to want to quit. Meanwhile, Tendi messes up someone’s ascension to a higher plane of existence and it’s a SERIOUS social faux pas. Yeah, I imagine.

Mariner makes her shitty jobs still somehow fun and loose and that can’t do, so they come up with a NEW plan to make her suffer. They promote her.

Now she gets to sit in on meetings about what kinda chairs we should have in the meeting room, and in on the friendly poker night :D Okay, this is an actually funny joke to me. ACTUALLY good conceit. Can’t wait to see how they fuck it up.

I don’t like the Tendi half though, it’s nothing but awkward humor. Though when she says “I HATE when people don’t like me, it’s like ants in my brain!” holy shit I feel that.

The mission they are ACTUALLY trying to do falls apart cause everyone is being a dick to each other and not paying attention to it, just trying to one up each other and focus on what THEY want not what needs to be done.

They manage to fix everything cause yeah, Mariner is actually good at her job she’s just kind of a dick. The guy Tendi’s been harassing ACTUALLY ascends. It’s horrifying. I think the people who got mutated in the transporter in the first movie suffered less.

The show makes fun of how people say Sensors in Star Trek. Has it earned that? Maybe. I kinda like the joke. Anyways Mariner is demoted again cause she makes fun of an admiral’s pronunciation of sensors, and the status quo is back.

Cupid’s Errant Arrow - ENT 3/10

Let’s play a game. Can you name every reference in this image?

The Cerritos is meeting up with some ship that everyone says is super cool and important for some reason. I dunno why but like, okay. Boimler’s girlfriend is on the ship and is visiting. Mariner is freaked out at first, but turns out, she is great! A little TOO GREAT. And we now have your stereotypical “jealous of your girlfriends’ ex” plotline which I dunno. Boimler is hyper focused on that, while Mariner is CERTAIN she’s a shape changing evil alien.

Meanwhile, we got B AND C plots. The captain is trying to convince weird conspiracy theory aliens to let htem blow up a moon that’ll kill them if they don’t. And Tendi and Rutherford are on the other starship getting jealous of how cool it is. Somehow.

There’s a joke where they talk about how great Trip Tucker was and it legit made us upset.

The “Boimler is jealous” jokes are EXACTLY what I feared them to be. Bad :) I do not like these kind of jokes or this kind of humor. Eventually they get past it.

This is an image of Boimer naked wanting to do sexy times. This episode moves too fast to write up stuff. It’s not very good or interesting anyway tbh. Turns out the aliens were just two rich eccentric assholes so they just ignore them and blow up the moon anyway, then Boimler’s girlfriend and Mariner bond over how lame and bad at everything Boimler is. Okay. Also Boimler was infected a parasite that made him irresitable. She goes out with the parasite instead.

Didn’t like this one much.

Cupid’s Errant Arrow - ENT 5.5/10

This episode introduces Fletcher, an old acadamy buddy of Boimler’s. He’s a nice guy, but is ever so slightly awkward.. He buddies up to Boimler and Mariner very quickly. Meanwhile, Rutherford and Tendi hang out in a holodeck training program.

Okay, a thing I’m noticing. Why is EVERY episode Boimler and Mariner team up, while Tendi and Rutherford team up? Can’t we like, split them up every now and then into different configurations?

Fletcher is attacked and some stuff is stolen. They don’t go to the officers cause why would they that’d be silly. He’s clearly making shit up to get out of fucking up.

Rutherford invented a training program AI called Badgey. It’s very clearly a Clippy thing, and is extremely sinister. I like it. When the ship is attacked, the holodeck loses its safety protocols and Badgey starts attacking violently. Uh, got our first Shit Goes Wrong In The Holodeck episode I guess.

Holy shit Badgey rips some heads off. Well, it is an “adult animation”, so that means it’s gotta have SOME weird gratuitous violence.

Whoops Feltcher got some brain damage and caused an evil thing to happen. Basically his personality is uploaded to a computer core. Turns out he’s kinda awful. They tie him up so he doesn’t get in the way. Fair. THey kick it out an airlock and it blows up the ship attacking them. Fletcher is promoted off the ship for his daring creativity and genius…. Ha ha??

Cupid’s Errant Arrow - ENT 6.5/10

Tendi re-created a dog from scratch. Ha ha nope it’s a horrifying spider monster. That actually made me giggle. And it’s still a cute perfect pupper :3 It turns into a cube. I want one. Tendi loves it regardless. Oh no, I AM Tendi aren’t I?

A sub captain comes to visit and take over for a while. Turns out it’s Mariner’s old best friend! Oh boy, surely this’ll be wonderful. Meanwhile Boimler wants to kiss up but he goes throuh some kind of phasic nonsense and is out of alignment. So they send him to The Farm upstate. No seriously it’s called the Farm. Okay good, this time Tendi and Boimler are together, good.

Meanwhile, Mariner goes down with the new captain on an away mission. She’s immediately incompetent and bad at everything and the rest of the captain’s crew doesn’t like her. What? She’s always been hyper competent that’s like her character trait. I think the idea is she FEELS inadequet next to her best friend who is now a captain but it doesn’t come through super well?

Turns out the Farm is where they send freaks they can’t fix so they don’t ruin the shiny prestige of Starfleet. They even have one of the fish lizards from the end of Threshold. The “freaks” as they call themselves plan a mutiny. The dude in charge finds out and charges to kill them all cause they have no rights. What the fuck is going on? Like yes it’s a joke and the series is hyperbolic about everything but this is like the most dark thing I’ve ever seen and they’re playing it VERY straight.

Okay turns out she’s INTENTIONALLY fucking up. She’s just a VERY good actor. She’s fucking up cause she doesn’t want to be forced into a command job. She just wants to be “a super good ensign”.

Okay turns out the farm is real everyone just completely misunderstood the situation cause the guy in charge has a short leash and is SUPER creepy and menacing. He says he’s working on it. I also like that he’s one of the three armed guys from TAS. Boimler loses his phasy-ness and is kicked out for not being a freak.

Mariner and the captain end on good terms. Mariner says she still has stuff to figure out. In the end I kinda liked this one? Has some weird problems, but in the end it twisted itself to some funny ends.

Veritas - ENT 4/10

The Lower Deckers are put in front of an alien tribunal to give testimony for the actions of the bridge crew. They demand that Mariner explains what happened on a certain stardate. In that date, they were on the bridge as some aliens were attacking over a misunderstanding about a map of the neutral zone. The tribunal doesn’t like this and asks Rutherford about another unrelated date.

It’s a bunch of weird unrelated plots. Basically any time Rutherford’s implant updates he passes out and it takes over, being super competent. But he blacks out a lot. So it jumps from scene to scene. Including a weird Gorn Wedding scene.

Tendi is super sus. She clearly knows something.

Yeah so basically she was accidentally roped into a commando bravo mission with Ransom. They’re all very Broohaha marine style about all this and going into the neutral zone for stupid nonsense.

She gets too into it and beats the shit out of some armed guards. The tribunal doesn’t believe her.

Boimler defends his bosses by saying yes they fuck up cause no one actually ever knows what they’re doing, and they’re NEVER told what’s happening ever. But they do their best. Turns out this guy holding them in trial, Clar, actually is just throwing a party for celebration of them. It’s weird.

I’m not sure about this one. Also it turned Q into Myzlplx for some reason.

Crisis Point - ENT 2/10

Mariner frees a species of lizard people from rat tyrany. Captain Freeman tells her off cause Prime Directive. Oh please do not tackle that topic you clearly can’t handle it. She gets sent off for therapy, cause it’s… bad to want to stop this? What the fuck is this show trying to say

No seriously, that’s a big problem. I’ve been wondering it quietly but Neli broached the subject. What is it TRYING to say? Starfleet bad? Starfleet good? It never seems to know.

Boimler makes a holodeck tool using the crew’s private logs so he can practice brown nosing them. Mariner hijacks it to turn it into a movie so she can let out frustrations. Boimler continues to try and brown nose. It’s awkward and embarassing. It’s filled with a lot of jokes about the way the TNG movies were filmed. It’s scarily accurate. But I didn’t LIKE those movies so…

Credit to all the lens flares and dimmer lighting though.

In the movie, Mariner plays a space warlord trying to take over the ship. Rutherford goes off to geek out with the Hologram Lead Engineer, while Tendi is like “what the fuck this is gross”. Mariner is ALSO racist about Orions, and Tendi eventually just walks out.

The ship crashes pretty much exactly like in Generations.

LOL okay good joke. “It’s okay sir I did a repeating beaming sequence and saved them all” “That’s literally impossible??” “No, it’s a movie, you can do whatever you want in a movie.” “Oh, okay!”

Mariner’s simulated self fights her real angry self.

What THE FUCK, THIS SHOW JUST DROPPED THE F SLUR. Rutherford went off with his boss after they confess their love for each other, and the doctor calls them the f-word. This is not a joke. Like, not in a bad way? But like…

no. You did not earn that. What in the fuck.

Anyways it all ends, Mariner feels better about it, says therapy works, and all’s good. Also Boimler finds out Mariner is the captain’s daughter, which is apparently a secret.

The memory alpha page says it says “fucks”. It’s bleeped. But it DEFINETLY sounds like ts at the end, not cks, and the lip flaps fit the f slur.

Okay, I found an uncensored version of the scene. She says “Godspeed, you crazy fucks” for sure. Holy SHIT it did not sound like that. And the context did not help. Okay. It’s fine then.

Anyways I still didn’t like this much. It just seemed kind of unhinged in a way I didn’t appreciate, cause it turns out her murdering everyone was good therapy and Tendi was wrong! What?

No Small Parts - ENT 3.5/10

This episode starts with them calling the TOS era the TOS era. It stands for Those Old Scientists. Alright. They just stopped Landru, from The Return of the Archons, from doing that thing he does. I don’t even think I remember that episode.

Anyways Boimler tells Mariner he knows her mom is her mom. Right as her mom is watching them and broadcasting them to the bridge. So now EVERYONE knows. Whoops!

:o there’s a new Exocomp on the crew. She’s a genki girl. I love her already. Her name is Peanut Hamper. Tendi is being weird about trying to make sure she has help. Is this a take on people with disabilities?

Everyone is suddenly EXTREMELY weird about Mariner. They’re giving her gifts and treating her extremely differently, so she wants to dip as soon as possible. But there’s no way she can transfer like this, so she decides to take things seriously and be a perfect little ensign for a while so she can get off the ship.

They beam in to help someone with what they think is routine, find the ship completely destroyed by some scary ship, and then nearly destroyed themselves. Who could this mystery villain be?? It’s… it’s… the Pacleds.

Yes.

THe fucking Pacleds. Remember them? They’re the ones who were a joke. Every alien is a “species of hats” and their hat is that they are supposed to be R-words. It was the 80s. They were a massive mistake. Anyways they’re here now, but they’re EXTREMELY menacing and evil instead. They were always morally bankrupt (ha ha, cause people with mental disabilities are actually evil). That whole thing is toned down, it’s not really here. But I can’t help but fucking think about it every time they’re back.

They literally go “wait but you’re a joke”. So it’s entirely on purpose.

They need Peanut Hamper to save the day. She says no, she’s not gonna sacrifice her life. I mean, fair, but she’s kinda a dick about it. She sayus she was just here to piss off her dad, and just dips.

Rutherford goes instead, with Shaxs the security officer, and uses a virus made by Badgey to blow them up. Badgey betrays them and Shaxs sacrifices himself to save Rutherford, dying in the process.

More Pacleds show up, but are scared off by… THE TITAN! Commanded by William Riker (who Mariner is friends with btw), who is cool and scares them off easily.

Rutherford lost his memory, but Tendi takes this as a time to become best friends all over again. Okay. I guess that’s a fun subversion of this thing. The captain makes Mariner her personal “you can do illegal things when I need them”. Her personal Section 31? That’s kinda fucked up. And Boimler uses all this to sneak a promotion over to the Titan, and Mariner is PISSED. The end.

I’m not sure I liked this season very much. But I’m not gonna write off this series yet, I hear it gets better. Here’s hopin’!

entry.title: Retro Achievement Mastery 221 - B3313
entry.content:
This is a big one. This is actually currently my biggest set I’ve mastered, and it’s not even close. The FF4 Four Job Fiesta set, which involves you beating the game three times, is 167 achievements. This is 298.

It is a BIG ONE.

It’s almost doing this game a disservice to review it. I could come on here and give you the True Lore Version. This is a lost copy of Mario 64 found at a garage sale, filled with unknown cursed evils, dive deep. In reality, it’s a romhack based on Mario 64 betas and creepy pastas. Like, obviously. It’s not ACTUALLY a cursed cartridge. But it’s trying to intentionally give that kind of energy, and it succeeds EXTREMELY well at that.

It starts off very simply. You start in a recreation of the Spaceworld demo that, for the longest time, we only had screenshots of. It’s a very nice recreation. You even have the weird beta sound effects that are just old loony toons screams and sped up Michael Jackson for some reason. But then as you collect stars, things… shift. The game constantly changes and shifts in ways that are almost imperceptible. The numbers on doors change, for seemingly no reason. Transitions are non-euclidian. You get a number of stars and now things change even MORE, and MORE, until by the end, your map looks like fucking nonsense, and you have over 400 stars. Yes, there are 450 stars in this hack. It’s a VERY large hack.

If you’re curious, here’s my map I made of this hack. Spoilers, obviously. I’m told this is the most readable map some people have seen. Most of them look like this!!

The most funp art of making your own map is you get to name each hub area based on what YOU think the most important landmarks are. I had lots like “beta lobby”, “vanilla upstairs”, but then I have ones like “Toad Long Hall”, “Slow Giant Castle”, “Red Fog Lobby”, and “Nazca Line Basement”.

There are a LOT of levels and areas to explore in B3313, and while not all of them are bangers, and some are actively annoying (looking at you, Bowser’s Factory), the game is genuinely something impressively magical, and I am straight up sad I will never be able to play it blind again.

There’s just a vibe coming off of B3313. It tastes like nostalgia that you’ve experienced and loved, but shifted, twisted. Changed by your memory, just ever so slightly off where you can convince yourself you dreamed that as a kid, or you had just forgotten. And I fucking love that feeling. I seriously can’t recommend it enough.

Oh, it IS important to note, it does have a few jump scares. If that’s a thing you dislike. There’s not many and they’re PRETTY obvious that they’ll be coming up.

entry.title: Retro Achievement Mastery 219 - Niñoid
entry.content:
Another day, another tiny homebrew that’s basically just a demo. This time it’s Niñoid, by Three Twins Games. It’s a metroidvania I decided to play while still kind of in a Metroid mood. I played it right after Junkoid, which I didn’t master, but might talk about someday.

Anyways, Niñoid wears its inspirations on its sleeve, as you can see from this screenshot. You play as a kid chasing after his cat. You go into some ancient cat temple and get cat themed upgrades to proceed. There are only like ten rooms. Like I said, it’s more of a demo than anything else. You can beat the game in under 2 minutes. But for whats here, it’s cool? I wish the devs would do more with this engine!

entry.title: Retro Achievement Mastery 220 - Ripple Island
entry.content:
In many ways, Ripple Island genuinely stuns me. It is one of the best looking Famicom games and is massively ahead of its time. In other ways, it’s massively OF its time. This is a 1988 Sunsoft visual novel. Sunsoft was really coming into their own around this time on the Famicom, and would make some of the very best action games. They’re well known for Blaster Master, Batman, Journey to Silius, Gimmick… not so much the visual novels. This only came out in Japan, and we sadly didn’t get it over here.

In Ripple Island you play as Kyle, a young boy who lives on the island. The evil Emperor Groaker uses magic to ravage the island and kidnap princess Nasarell. The king declares that whoever shall stop the fiend will get Nasarell’s hand in marriage, so Kyle sets off to save his home. Along the way you travel through a beautiful and varied island, talk to a bunch of cute bunnies, and team up with a girl named Cal.

A LOT of Japanese visual novels of the time had the same gameplay loop. You had to talk to every single character, exhaust all dialogue trees, look at everything, touch everything, then you could move on, Ripple Island is NOT like that. It’s a lot more like a classic Western point and click, than anything. You explore a small confined area, grab items, use them against other items or objects in the scenery, solve simple minor puzzles, and then move on.

Ripple Island takes place over five chapters, which are their own contained little areas. This helps keep it segmented and doesn’t let it get super convoluted or bloated. The areas are nice, varied, and have some good puzzles.

To really enjoy Ripple Island, you’d need to like that classic adventure style of game. As the world’s biggest Zork fan, I do, in fact, like that style of game. But it’s not for everyone. Despite that, I think its charm really shines through. It’s not very long, and rather easy for this style of adventure game, so I do highly recommend it.

entry.title: Retro Achievement Mastery 217 - Final Fantasy 1 - Economic Collapse

link.title: Retro Achievement Mastery 217 - Final Fantasy 1 - Economic Collapse

entry.content:
So, I’ve beaten FF1 a few times now. Already mastered it, even. So what’s this, exactly? Well, this is a romhack of FF1 where it’s… FF1 again, but there’s no stores.

That’s it.

So basically, it’s FF1. But okay, so the way it works is this: the game is unbelievably rough in the first 2/5ths or so, until you get enough items from dungeons that the last 3/5ths are just Final Fantasy 1. But UNTIL then, no pure potions, no potions in general, if you’re inflicted with Stone you should just reload frankly.

Is this worth playing over FF1? No, obviously not. Is it even worth playing if you just wanna replay FF1? Uh, well… if you really like FF1? Which I do. I just kinda wanted an NES RPG to grind out while watching Youtube videos. Which this is even more that than FF1. So, hell yea. Got what I wanted.

entry.title: Retro Achievement Mastery 218 - Idol Hakkenden
entry.content:
Idol Hakkenden, also translated as “Pop Star Debut”, is a Japanese only visual novel about a failgirl daughter of a millionaire who wants to be a pop idol. In order to inheret her family fortune over her actually successful sisters, she has to become a popular idol as soon as possible. To do this, she has to like… fulfill some prophecy given to her by a fortune teller where she has to find a posse of friends, travel the world, and stop a horrible evil (bad comedy)

This game is obviously ridiculous. It is not serious in any way, but even beyond that, it’s absolutely buck fucking wild. I think the wildest part might be the time you have to talk a suicide bomber down from blowing up a concert full of people by singing a song to him?

Yeah uh, this game has that!

Other than the wild premise and even wilder writing, this game is a pretty bog-standard 80s/early 90s Japanese visual novel. All games in this genre were like this back then. You had a couple of commands, usually look, talk, use, etc. You need to do all of the commands possible in every area, then move on. This is a BIT more involved than that? You have to like, solve puzzles and use tone indicators (aka, act dumb) to get what you want a lot of the time.

Honestly this game is best played with a guide. But if you DO play it like that, it’s a two hour romp through some WILD bullshit that’s extremely entertaining. I was not expecting to enjoy this that much? I kinda famously hate idol culture, and this is literally about that, but its just so stupid that I can’t help but love it.

entry.title: LizstarTrek - Discovery Season 1 episodes 11-15
entry.content:
Every Sunday my Twitch subs and I watch Star Trek in my Discord. Here are my reviews and thoughts on each of the episodes as I see it. If you’re curious about a nerd’s views on Star Trek episodes, please read on, I go quite in depth with some of these episodes! I’ve watched some Star Trek before but not all of it!

I know literally nothing about Discovery, except for The Burn, and that it leads into Strange New Worlds. So uh… I’ve been dreading this one. But hey, who knows! Maybe it’ll surprise me!!

I am going to become the joker :)

The Wolf Inside - ENT 2.5/10

There is a wolf inside you. The furry convention is going well.

The power went out and no one went to check on medbay??? We have patients?!

We have a big voice over from Michael about how THE LIGHT HAS GONE OUT FROM THIS WORLD. CAN YOU HIDE YOUR HUMANITY? YOUR DECENCY? CAN YOU SURVIVE BEFORE IT EATS YOU? Holy fuck I miss DS9’s Mirror episodes so god damn badly. Remember when the mirror universe was goofy fun, but also dark deep down where it y’know, hits you but isn’t just grimdark edgy nonsense? Fucking CHRIST. We watch them murder people by beaming them into the vacuum of space, we watch them freeze and watch the air fly out of their lungs…. THEN WE WATCH BURNHAM AND TYLER HAVE NAKED SNUGGLES.

WHAT FUCKING TONE ARE YOU TRYING TO REACH?

Oh hey they finally found out that the doctor was killed. They just decide it was Stamets in his comotose state. Somehow. There are… SO MANY WAYS to find out what actually happened. Check the DNA on his neck. Check memory engrams. Fucking say “Hey computer, who was the last person to enter sick bay”. YOU SHOULD BE MORE WORRIED ABOUT THIS.

Anyways Burnham gets orders to blow up a resergant camp. She claims she’ll go down and take them out by herself for the glory. She then surrenders to them IMMEDIATELY and begs them to let her save them, which they obviously are uninterested in hearing, but after Mirror Sarek comes out and mind melds with her, they immediately change their plans. The leader of this group is Voq, remember him? The pale Klingon? He’s speaking in Klingon and it’s freaking Tyler out.

Tyler goes nuts and attacks Voq. They SOMEHOW manage to make it out of this okay? Oh also there’s lots of stuff about Burnham asking Voq why he’s teamed up with all these other peoples, so she can figure out how to get the Klingons to the peace table back home. Uh… cause otherwise it’s death?? Burnham confronts Tyler about this, and he FINALLY awakens, and it turns out… he’s Voq! Who was turned into a Human through a violent ritual, in order to infiltrate the ship. He DOES have the “real” Tyler’s memories and thoughts but he’s also Voq. He attemts to kill her but is stopped by Mirror Saru, who Burnham was kind to earlier, so he saved her.

This is poorly written, but it’s hard to explain why. You just need to see it. Anyways he’s beamed off to die and then Discovery scoops him up and puts in him chains.

They kill Stamets. He dies. All our gays, buried :D But apparently for some reason he’s still KIND OF alive… in the mushroom forest dimension! This looks like a fucking Disney movie.

Another ship comes and blows the fuck out of the rebels. Oh no, who would do such a thing?! It’s the Emperor… Georgiou!! Remember her? She was Michael’s captain. Oh wowee what a twist, what fun. I guess this means Georgiou is like, Hoshi’s grand daughter? Cool.

Vaulting Ambition - ENT 2.5/10

Stamets is in the Magical Fungal Forest, talking to his mirror self, who is the most pleasant mirror universe guy I’ve EVER met. But also there’s something “bad” going on, “corrupting” the fungus. Trust The Fungus I guess.

Meanwhile Burnham takes Lorca to the Emperor, who is VERY happy Burnham is okay, calling her daughter. A bit after caressing her in a very Toxic Mommy Empress kinda way. Damn, killed that one before I could enjoy it.

Anyways turns out Mirror Burnham betrayed the empress so she sentences her to death too. Whoops! But she convinces the Empress not to kill her by covincing her she’s from the Prime Universe. The Empress is like “Huh, how quaint, parallel universes?” and then kills all of her primary guard except for one with the fucking thing that blue guy from Guardians of the Galaxy uses. What the fuck. And then she makes the last guard alive the “Duke of Andor”. Which is VERY funny as a Trek Nerd, because Andor is the gas giant that Andoria revolves around. lol. Duke of a gas giant. Thank you, my empress, how… kind.

Stamets finds out that Culber is dead, because apparently a ghost image of him was dragged into the Fungus Network Forest. Okay. His fucking boyfriend became a force ghost leading him back to his body. Also apparently Mirror Stamets did evil shit to the mycellial network and corrupted it. This killed all the fungus on the Discovery, so they’re VERY stuck now.

The Empress talks to Burnham about what Mirror Lorca was apparently like… He was her father figure, but groomed her. They literally use that word, groomed. It was… gross. All the things she explains HOW he groomed her, we get twists from past scenes showing he’s STILL doing it. This is how Burnham realizes the Twist.

LORCA IS FROM THE MIRROR UNIVERSE.

I guessed this a long-ass time ago btw. And immediately after she realizes this, Lorca puts on the Villain Hat VERY easily, getting out of the excrutiator, and killing people. Better late than never I guess.

Lorca being evil is a twist that like… makes you recontextualize this series a little bit. Cause like, he was ALWAYS bad and evil all along, so all the evil shit he’s doing is justified, right? No. Obviously not. He’s been in the prime universe for a long time and they just Let Him Do That. It was never treated as bad. That’s the problem.

What’s Past Is Prologue - ENT 3/10

Lorca frees all of his crew off screen. Why are they here on this ship? Why were they not killed? THey also find Stamets. Uh, why is he awake??? What? Did THAT also happen off screen?

The Emperor is annoyed at the Lorca stuff, cause he ends up releasing a bioweapon into the airvents and killing lots of people. She decides to punish Burnham for this, who goes gungho and escapes a room with 40 guards. Okay. How? Whatever.

We need GALACTIC LEVEL STAKES so yes, the corruption in the mycelial network will kill everyone. EVERYONE. In every single timeline. In all universres. Total existance wipeout. Okay. We totally needed that. This wasn’t big enough already.

Lorca kills Mirror Stamets just for the fuck of it, and then tries to groom Burnham into joining his side. She just turns off his signal and ignores him. He’s like “oh she’ll come around.” Yeah okay dude. Fucking creep.

Burnham has one last trick up her sleeve if she wants to save everyone and stop Lorca, cause fuck that guy. She’s gonna use herself as bait. And since Lorca seems to be fucking obsessed with her, he takes the bait. I don’t… RELALY know what her plan her eis but hey, he’s into it.

They do a coordinated attack, Michael and the Empress inside the throne room and the Discovery firing upon the ship.

“Hey why isn’t Georgiou using that thing the blue guy from Guardians of the Galaxy-“ “That was a previous scene, Liz.” “Oh, my bad.”

They kill Lorca, and Georgiou tries to die her own way, but is taken against her will onto the Discovery. They blow up the thing killing the mycellium, then use the explosion as a wave to ride back to their timeline. They get back… but they overshot by nine months apparently. So the Klingons have won the war. Yay.

The fight scene was cool I guess.

What’s Past Is Prologue - ENT 1/10

Empress Georgiou IMMEDIATELY calls Saru a slave and tries to stungun him. What the fuck did you THINK was gonna happen??

That one admiral who fucked Lorca once forces her way onto the ship at gun point. Then they have Sarek force a mind meld onto Saru which is NOT… how thats supposed to work. But that means this works out very fast. They explain that the Klingons lost ALL leadership and are now basically 22 houses. So they are disordered, and that means they are even scarier than usual. Wh-what? That’s not… thats not how that……. okay.

Tyler is back to normal. They did a procedure that fixes him. He has all of Voq’s memories but that guy is basically dead. Okay. Everyone is disgusted that he’s here, and even though Saru won’t arrest him because HE didn’t do anything, it was Voq, he’s kinda mistreated. Wow what a surprise.

God, yes, show, we get it. War is bad. Holy shit. It’s bad and grim and people die.

This shit is exhausting to watch.

At least House D’Gor has this starbase now. He’s the dude Quark humiliates in DS9. Thats a reference, I guess. I am so checked out, y’all.

Burnham asks Georgiou how they defeated the Klingons, so she tells her. They took the fight to the Klingon homeworld and made them submit. She takes that info to the Admiral, and they formulate a plan. They’re just going to carpet bomb Q’onos :)

One admiral literally says “Federation tactics have failed us”. Holy fucking christ. Also, THAT’S your new level tactics? JUST BLOW UP THE CAPITAL AND THE ENTIRE MILITARY IN ONE ATTACK? OH WOW, YEAH. THAT’S A GENIUS STATEGY WHY DIDN’T WE THINK OF THAT.

Sarek: “Perhaps you loving the enemy is a sign of what makes us great. Anyways let’s go carpet bomb their capital.”

They terraform some dead planet and turn it into a fungus planet. Uh, okay. That was easy. Its that easy to terraform moons.

I’m fucking sorry???

What the actual shit

Starfleet enlists Empress Georgiou, making her take the identity of the dead Georgiou. She takes over Discovery. What the fuck did Saru do to deserve this?!

Will You Take My Hand? - ENT 2/10

No. I will not.

Georgiou is very uh… crude and evil. Obviously. She’s like “Do not call it the Klingon homeworld. They are animals. Call it the planet.” This’d be better if 90% of the bridge crew didn’t serve under her and know what kind of person she is. Why the fuck is this happening.

People, again, told me “Lorca being evil all along recontextualizes the evil shit they do earlier in the season”. THEN WHAT IS THIS? Oh but they’re losing the war, their values are being tested. WELL THEY FUCKING FAILED THE TEST. Yes, when the Federation was losing the war, Sisko did a bad evil thing. But he did one bad evil thing. They didn’t go “let’s listen to the fascist and give them full power and control over our super weapon.”

They go on an away mission down to Qo’onos, hiding in the planet’s core or some shit, and then sneak into an Orion embassy. Tilly is here! The one really actually kinda funny moment in this entire show is Tilly going “omg Georgiou I’m so happy to finally meet you, I’m so glad you survived!” “Ahh, Killy. What fun we had, subjugating the Andorians.” “Oh, she’s not… okay.”

Georgiou is bisexual, taking two sex slaves, one guy, one girl. Okay cool, I guess. At least THAT’S accurate to Mirror Lore.

Tyler is there using his Voq Knowledge to help them. He gambles and speaks Klingon. This causes Michael to relapse into her racism. Cool.

Oh goodie, we find out that Georgiou is actually using this to plant a “hydro-bomb” in the planet and blow it up. Tilly finds out about it, and then Georgiou shows up and knocks her out.

Then it goes to commercial break, comes back, and Tilly is talking to Tyler and Burnham about this. I… what? What the FUCK happened?

I think I realize why this admiral never realizes that Lorca had been replaced by a fascist monster. Also WHAT THE FUCK

If this season does not end with 60% of Starfleet being tried for warcrimes, it will be a failure.

Burnham convinces them maybe don’t do a genocide. And then Burnham goes down to the planet and is like “hey Georgiou, please don’t” and she goes “fine whatever”. They then give the bomb to L’Rell?? She’s gonna use it to become ruler of the Klingons or something? This is very dumb. “I won’t shoot you, that’d be evil. But I WILL give this gun to my friend and SHE might shoot you :)”

Everything works out. Yay. Also Tyler leaves with L’Rell, maybe he can do some good for both sides. Whatever. Burnham is reinstated, Tilly is promoted, and they’re going to Vulcan to get their new captain. But on the way, they get a distress call from… Captain Pike of the Starship Enterprise! Oh boy!

This is the worst season of Star Trek so far, and I don’t think I need to explain why.

entry.title: Retro Achievement Mastery 216 - Ace Attorney
entry.content:
So, I’d never actually beaten Ace Attorney 1. I decided to fix that!

Ace Attorney is an extremely classic and beloved franchise that I really do not need to explain. You play (in the first 3 games at least) as Phoenix Wright, a newbie defense attorney. You defend a motley crew of characters, face off against an intruiging cast of prosecuting attorneys, interrogate and find the hidden truths behind testimonies, and often times topple some kind of conspiracy.

The writing and characters are often the reason to play Ace Attorney, and I don’t disagree. This is, at its core, basically a visual novel with a few little mini games built in. ESPECIALLY this version, which is a suped up version of the GBA game with an additional case tacked on with lots of DS stuff so that you get your DS moneys worth. For Japanese fans who already had the original, it prolly felt like a fun DLC or expansion pack mission. For us fans in America who had never played one of these games before this point, it was really weirdly paced and long compared to the rest of the cases. And also easily ignorable in the context of the series despite seeming to be VERY important (it’s a giant police conspiracy). But that’s fine.

I don’t want to go super in depth about this game, because it comes highly recommended. Every Ace Attorney game has like, 3 fantastic cases and 1 mediocre one, though. And for the most part I really like this game, but it certainly has its slow spots. Case 3 is that, in my opinion, but that’s fine.

I also started Ace Attorney 2, but at the time of writing this, I have not finished it, because I dreaded the fucking clown case so much I never even started it. I really should get around to that one of these days…

entry.title: LizstarTrek - Discovery Season 1 episodes 6-10
entry.content:
Every Sunday my Twitch subs and I watch Star Trek in my Discord. Here are my reviews and thoughts on each of the episodes as I see it. If you’re curious about a nerd’s views on Star Trek episodes, please read on, I go quite in depth with some of these episodes! I’ve watched some Star Trek before but not all of it!

I know literally nothing about Discovery, except for The Burn, and that it leads into Strange New Worlds. So uh… I’ve been dreading this one. But hey, who knows! Maybe it’ll surprise me!!

IT HASN’T SO FAR! :’)

Lethe - ENT 2/10

What the fuck is this Starwars Episode 2 shit

Sarek and some other Vulcan are doing some diplomatic mission, blasting off of… I GUESS it’s supposed to be Vulcan. Which for some reason has four moons in the sky. (it canonically has none)

There is no fucking way this black project secret weapon ship has BRANDED T-SHIRTS, WHICH THE CREW ARE RANDOMLY WEARING DURING WORKOUTS. And apparently we have holodecks now?! Those don’t exist- ughhhh. If this review was just me talking about anochronistic shit where the people making this clearly knew nothing about Star Trek, it’d be all of the review.

I have to say, this clearly does not want to be Star Trek, and if it was it’s own new IP, it’d be bad, but ignorable. As it is, it’s a black fucking stain.

The Vulcan sidekick Sarek was going with is actually an extremist who is here to assassinate Sarek. He turns himself into a bomb and blows himself up, saying that Vulcans are superior to Humans and soon they were realize and withdraw from the Federation.

It’s fascinating that we already dealt with this in Enterprise, but we’re going at it in the exact opposite direction. We ALREADY went over this y’all, don’t need to retread THIS stupid plot point.

Burnham has a weird Force Diad Attack and is forced to watch something in Sarek’s mind, which makes him angry and he violently kicks her out. She comes to and is like “Sarek is in trouble, captain, you have to help me find him”. Oh yeah sure how fucking hard can it be, SPACE ISN’T LARGE OR ANYTHING

WHAT THE FUCK ARE LOGIC EXTREMISTS

“We’ll send out a probe” OH, YOU HAVE PROBES NOW. Maybe they were invented in the last six months after the fuck up of the first episode. Anyways, Starfleet is PISSED at Captain Lorca cause he’s launching an unauthorized rescue mission with a convicted felon and a POW who has JUST recovered. They send an admiral, who admonishes him until he goes “You know we could be having sex right now” and then they start fucking. Damn. Is anyone else wet or is it just me?

Anyways we find out that many years earlier, Burnham didn’t graduate and wasn’t allowed into an institute of science. She lived with the idea that she had failed for years and Starfleet was the second best thing she could do. But it turns out that Sarek is living with guilt about this, because oh wow, the people in charge were racist! Racist vulcans oh boy, MORE OF THAT. They’re like “You also want Spock to join us, and we can’t have TWO non-Vulcans, that would be illogical because I said it is.”

WRITE VULCANS RIGHT CHALLENGE FAILED

Lorca pulls a phaser on his admiral girltoy in bed and she’s like “What the FUCK”. She deems him unfit for command and says she’ll take his command away from him. He is, for the rest of the episode, SUPER sussy and it’s obvious what’s about to happen.

The episode ends with the Admiral going to some peace talks with some Klingons and being captured. Wow what a fucking shock. NOW, when finding out she’s been captured, Lorca is like “Well it might be a trap we should be careful before going in for a rescue. Let’s await orders.” Aight dude.

Once again, not good.

Magic to Make the Sanest Man Go Mad - ENT 0/10

What a pretentious fucking title. Anyways, this is THE episode to me. Like, when I first heard about Discovery, I heard some of the things they did. And this was the first thing I remember hearing about, and it’s stuck with me the entire time. Just a fucking shock of “Why the FUCK would they do that to an established character???? This is straight up character assassination”

Let’s begin.

Burnham is crushing on Tyler but trying to ignore it because of trauma. Please ignore the fact that they’ve shared three scenes together, and in one of them, she was unconscious.

There’s a fucking frat party on the Discovery. What the hell?

Tilly is like “Yo what’s the deal with you two?” Uh. What? And then the doctor and engineer, I forget their names, are like “Yo what’s the deal with you two?” THEY ATE LUNCH TOGETHER TWICE? WHAT IS THIS WRITING?!

A single person sneaks on board through a space whale and takes out literally 8 people all by himself. He’s wearing a big bad beetle borg outfit for some reason. When he reveals who he is, it’s HARRY FUCKING MUDD. HARRY MUDD JUST KILLED 8 PEOPLE BY HIMSELF? HE’S A FAT USED CAR SALESMAN, HE’S NOT A FUCKING MARINE. He’s pissed that they left him on a prison ship and intends to sell the Discovery, but first needs to learn about it. He blows the ship up, killing everyone.

We’re back at the party. Oh boy. Time loop. Everything proceeds the same, except Stamets (I remembered his name cause Tyler said it, thanks Tyler) seems to remember the last loop and is ranting and raving about it. Everyone ignores him, though he knows how it starts and warns them to avoid the space whale, so now Tyler and Burnham are on red alert. Mudd beams into Engineering and tries to hijack the ship this time. You know, I think the Enterprise team is no longer the worst crew in space. These guys are bad at their fucking jobs. Anyways the ship explodes again after Stamets kills Mudd.

Stamets has been reliving this over and over, it’s not actually the third loop. It’s many, many loops, and Stamets is having a hard time keeping it all straight (ha ha get it). Anyways this episode is actually a comedy, did you know? Did you know it’s FUNNY to have Harry Mudd torture the crew, killing them over and over again in a stable time loop? It actually shows Mudd going through time loops, over, and over, and over, shooting and killing Lorca in so many inventive ways, with no care or emotion about it over than slight joy. And why? Cause they left him behind in a cell, because he had betrayed them.

What in the shit.

Also speaking of good writing, they go “YOU’RE MAD!” and he goes “No! I’m Mudd :D”. FANTASTIC. THANK YOU FOR THAT.

Anyways, Stamets finds a way to get Burnham to immediately believe him, and he needs her to get Tyler on board so he can give info about Mudd, cause they were cellmates. But she has no idea how to talk to people, so Stamets has to teach her how to talk to someone who likes her. This episode might have a tone problem. Sorry, that’s an understatement. This episode injects tone directly into its veins to get high and its tearing this family apart.

They make out. Again, in this timeline, they have spent two scenes together. She manages to convince Tyler, and they go to try and stop Mudd but fail, and Tyler is killed violently.

Stamets gives up, and is like “okay I can’t watch you kill more people, I’m the key you want to use the ship”. You couldn’t have waited for the NEXT LOOP so everyone’d still be alive?! They find out that Harry is hiding his time machine in the space whale, and ends up telling Mudd “hey, you actually want me alive because the Klingons wants me cause I killed their messaiah. Baiiii~” then fucking kills herself in front of him. What the fuck.

Again this is supposed to be a comedy.

Anyways this time they trick him and he summons his wife who he ran away from. Remember her form TOS? He made an android of her that always yells and nags. Here she is. Looking ab it nicer than that version. Anyways I’m not going to explain how they trick him because even this, me not trying to explain anything, is less lazy writing than the episode’s writing. Anyways he’s married to the ol’ ball and chain and Tyler and Burnham become A Thing or something.

I want to raise a glass. A GLASS! TO STAR TREK DISCOVERY! FOR MAKING THE SECOND 0/10 EPISODE I’VE EVER REVIEWED. How in the FUCK. I didn’t even LOVE Mudd, he’s not like a cherished character or anything (though I like I, Mudd a lot). But this is STRAIGHT UP a character assassination. He was a shitty conman, but he wouldn’t just kill and torture people. But he sure does here! He’s straight up sociopathic.

Every single decision in this episode is wrong. The actual conceit (I love time loops but man this is incoherent and impossible to follow), the writing (NO, I’M MUDD :D), the fucking characterization, DO I NEED TO EXPLAIN, the Comedy (No), the romance (VERY no)? I will say, Mudd’s actor? Very good. His acting and mannerisms are good and correct! But the material he has is bad bad very bad not good very bad.

Si Vis Pacem, Para Bellum - ENT 4/10

Stamets is starting to have some negative effects from using the warp drive. I can’t imagine why.

They go to some planet to do some science shit to learn more about the science behind the Klingons’ cloaking technology. While hiking to the thing they’re hiking to they’re suddenly interrupted by some Forest Energy that floats around them and tries to get them to follow it. Oh no, elf time. Time to be lead off a cliff. Anyways the Chorus here, whatever it is, is friendly, but now they can’t actually do their Science Work without its permission.

Meanwhile we get some torture scenes with Admiral uhh… I looked her up, Cornwell. She’s the one who was kidnapped two episodes ago. She’s being tortured… so it seems. Her torturer actually wants to escape with the admiral. Her name is L’Rell, and we actually saw her ages ago with that albino Klingon who swore to hunt down Burnham? Yeah we still have that plot point going. THough honestly I had to look up who she was, she seemed important but it’s impossible to recognize these Klingons with all that makeup.

Saru makes friends with these aliens, the Pahvan. They are very friendly and want to meet aliens. They also seem to kind of… genetically change Saru so he’s no longer genetically afraid of everything. Oh that’s weird. He’s also acting very, very sus suddenly.

Okay so uhhh, this episode was actually VERY Trek-like for a bit and I was kind of happy about that? But was expecting the other shoe to drop and for these Pahvan to actually be Evil. But they’re not! They’re very friendly and want to be buddies, which is very Trek! But because this show is NOT Trek, at all, it clashes, and it clashes VIOLENTLY when the plot has to come crashing back down to where it is. Anyways Saru basically intentionally strands them there so that they won’t bring the war to this planet. Fair tbh. I sure do love that our GOOD GUYS Are going to try and stop him from protecting this planet, so they can kill people more efficiently.

DS9 had war stories too where characters did bad things for the greater good, but the difference is those were written well.

L’Rell and Cornwell are trying to escape but are caught, and they have to act that they’re fighting. L’Rell SEEMINGLY fucking kills Cornwell? I don’t think so but it’s actually very confusing and hard to follow.

Saru attacks Burnham as she tries to send a distress beacon. The Pahvan see that they’re fighting, and Burnham pleads to them to help them see cloaked Klingon ships using their space crystal magic or whatever. The Pahvan light up the beacon and then everyone is beamed back to Discovery. So uh… are they going to arrest Savu now for mutiny?? No of course not. That’s only when Burnham does it.

Anyways turns out the Pahvans actually changes the signal as an INVITATION TO THE KLINGONS so that they can all be friends :D Ha ha, this stupid friendly species wants to be friends, don’t they understand we’re at war and have to KILL THEM? Stupid naive aliens. Burnham swears to protect them, then it ends.

This is the best episode cause it almost feels Trek at some points.

Into the Forest I Go - ENT 5/10

Discovery is ordered to leave and run away so that the Klingon ship doesn’t blow it the fuck up. The Captain accepts and leaves… or well, pretends to. He has no intention of actually doing that. Is this guy heroic or not?? Meanwhile, Stamets is being a Sussy Baka about his spore drive and doesn’t want to see the doctor, but the captain says it’s mandatory. And then when the doctor says “shit’s bad we need to take him off the navigation”, Lorca ignores it and tells him to keep jumping anyway. He also shows Stamets that, through some analysis, he’s determined that the mushroom drive might be able to jump into parallel universes. Which Stamets is like “that’s cool, I’m in”.

They come up with a plan, boarding the Klingon ship and disabling it. They’re gonna send Burnham cause hey, when the war is over she’s going back to jail anyway so who cares if she dies. For the record she asked for this, no one really thinks of her like that other than her. Tyler goes with her cause of course he does.

They find the admiral stuck in a room, alongside L’Rell, who had been thrown in there and was dying. She’s like “oh it’s you” to Tyler, who shoots her and goes into a PTSD panic attack. Gee maybe we shouldn’t have put a Klingon torture participent on this fucking ship?

The strategy is working, though. Discovery is jumping around like nuts, easily defeating the Klingon ship. It’ll take a lot of jumps to fully scan the ship and defeat the cloak or whatever, which is straight up going to kill Stamets.

The Klingons are like “this shit sucks let’s hit the bricks” and Burnham starts fighting to distract them. She announces “I KILLED YOUR MESSIAH AND YOU ARE A FAKER” to this guy who took over the messiah’s ship.

Oh, they’re fighting now! Who’s ready for some quick cuts and shitty editing that makes you sick?! :D

She manages to distract them long enough for the jumps to finish. They can now see the Klingons, and beam everyone back on board, including T’Rell for some reason. The Klingon leader is killed. The war is over. Yay!

Extremely hard cut after the ship explodes. It’s almost comical.

Well okay it’s not OVER yet but it’s a serious victory and prospects are good. Starfleet is calling Discovery home and awarding Lorca with a medal of honor.

We get a scene between Tyler and Burnham. It’d supposed to be very emotional. It is not, because their chemistry is like two noble gasses hangin’ out.

Holy lens flare, Batman. Stamets agrees to one last jump cause after this he’s retiring. Cool. Stamets is like “let me jump us home faster it’s fine”

We suddenly get a weird nightmare sex scene of Tyler fucking a Klingon. What is even going on in this show.

They jump and uh, it goes bad. Stamets screams, and passes out. They beam to some kind of wreckage, boom, episode end.

Despite Yourself - ENT 4.5/10

Things are weird and funky. A Vulcan ship fires on them, and another ship, the Cooper, scares them off. Stamets is also unresponsive so they’re a little dead in the water right now. Lorca posits it first: they are no longer in their universe.

Ahh man.

Burnham makes the suggestion that they check the logs. Lorca shuts that down and says it’s just cause he pushed Stamets too hard. Hmm.

Okay, I have to say, there’s something we’ve been noticing about this series. This ship feels weirdly empty. We have like, no crew other than tyhe main crew. There’s a few people on the bridge who are here all the time but they NEVER speak. The medical bay is super empty. Lorca is like “I’m putting another doctor in charge of Stamets”, and we were stunned cause there are other doctors here??? They keep sending Tyler out to do things with Klingon stuff even though he’s suffering from PTSD. Are there NO OTHER security officers here?

Tyler goes to L’Rell and lets her out of her cell when she asks him to. Where are the other security officers?! WHY IS NO ONE PROTECTING THE BRIG? Anyways she says something in Klingon and he awakens like a sleeper agent. But it seems to not work perfectly and he runs off, freaking out, leaving her back in the brig. I’m sure he’s gonna go tell everyone exactly what’s going on :D

They scan some data cores and find out what’s going on. They’re in the mirror universe. Oh boy! We’ve ALREADY done a First Contact Was Actually Earlier plot for the mirror universe, weird that we’re pushing this.

A ship comes along and wants to talk to Discovery. They found out Tilly was the captain of the Mirror Discovery so ha ha, sudden weird tone switch as she has to be awkward at communicating. Torca pretends to be a Scottish engineer. Ha ha :) They have not earned that joke.

They also find out that the Discovery came over here in that one Enterprise episode. Without a spore drive. So there’s SOME way back home, let’s find out what it is. THIS they continuity, huh? Okay. You’d think it’d be uh, hidden in some way? Cause they erased all data on that? I dunno.

Damn, Burnham is pretty hot like that

OH, OKAY. TYLER FUCKING MURDERS DOCTOR CULBUR’S NECK. This is why we should have OTHER DOCTORS IN THE MEDBAY? SOMEONE SHOULD HAVE SEEN THAT. Culbur is like “I think there’s another personality under you, something bad happened- oh I’m dead”

That’s one gay buried :D

Burnham goes to her ship that she’s supposed to be captain of, with Tyler (who’s TOTALLY FINE) and Lorca as prisoner, pretending to be her mirror self who died years earlier, back from hiding. Her second in command, now captain, attempts to murder her. It fails. Afterwards, Burnham returns to her quarters and meets with Tyler who SHOULD be like “Hey you’re not like, attached to Doctor Culbur right?” but isn’t even gonna fucking mention it. Do you think they’ve even found his corpse back on Discovery?

It cuts from Burnham and Tyler making out to Lorca being tortured in an excrutiator. This is almost comical.

This episode was almost okay.

entry.title: LizstarTrek - Discovery Season 1 episodes 1-5
entry.content:
Every Sunday my Twitch subs and I watch Star Trek in my Discord. Here are my reviews and thoughts on each of the episodes as I see it. If you’re curious about a nerd’s views on Star Trek episodes, please read on, I go quite in depth with some of these episodes! I’ve watched some Star Trek before but not all of it!

I know literally nothing about Discovery, except for The Burn, and that it leads into Strange New Worlds. So uh… I’ve been dreading this one. But hey, who knows! Maybe it’ll surprise me!!

The Vulcan Hello - ENT 3/10

We get some Klingon talking a bunch of nonsense. Hey, question, why the fuck do the Klingons look like that?

Okay, I looked it up. Apparently, the makeup designer guy they got for this, who had never done Trek before, wanted to make them more birdlike.

….I have more questions now. That’s supposed to be a BIRD?!

Anyways, we are introduced to two characters. Michael Burnham and her superior officer, Captain Georgiou. They’re stuck on a planet with no way of escaping, but manage to send basically a smoke signal to their ship with a giant Federation symbol. Well, glad they saw that through the thick cloud layer I guess.

Okay, immediate first thing I notice is that this show’s editing is… bad. Why are half the shots at a dutch angle? Is this a HORROR Trek show? And why is it cutting from shot to shot every second and a half? I’m trying to watch the fucking show, why the hell are we JUMPIN’ so much?!

Anyways there’s something that maybe did some bad thing to the ships sensors or a satellite or something, and the ship’s science officer, Saru, wants to check it out. They can’t send a shuttle for some reason so they send Burnham out in a space suit. I mean sure, you don’t have a shuttle whatever, but why don’t you just use a probe?

I’m already telling a couple poroblems here. One, this show has a serious addiction to Trying To Be Different from the rest of Trek. It takes place ten years before TOS, but it’s like nothing else. And like, I don’t mind that. Voyager was like “let’s do entirely new stuff not connected to anything else”. But the fucking show is DIRECTLY connected to the rest of them. But the problem is, there was weird contractural nonsense behind the scenes. They had no idea what this show as going to be while it was being made. Either based on the movies, or based on the prime show timeline. They were afraid to make a choice so they just… did a third thing and then awkwardly snapped it onto the prime timeline later anyway.

Anyways Burnham is attacked by a Klingon, and manages to kill them while almost dying herself. We learn from a flashback that Burnham was raised by Vulcans, specifically Serak. She’s very bad at being like a Vulcan, she’s VERY emotional. She’s rescued and revived, and her injury is kind of ignored from this point on. Anyways, a Klingon ship decloaks and menaces but doesn’t actually contact them. Apparently Klingons haven’t been seen for nearly 100 years. What. Also Burnham is being racist. “The Klingons are violent and evil” “You’re being racist? Dude it’s the 2240s, we’re past that.” and of course she’s going to end up being right isn’t she. Aaaaghhhhh. Also BTW her parents were killed by Klingons.

She talks to Sarek through a call. Hey, remember how DS9 made a big deal about hologram communication becoming a thing? It’s here, a hundred something years earlier. He explains how the Vulcans started diplomacy with the Klingons. They fought them. She’s like “oh good an excuse to shoot the Klingons” and is VERY behind that idea. So uh, this is our hero? Our good guy? She’s very flawed. Let’s hope she improve- and she attacks and knocks out the captain. She then mutinies and commands the ship to shoot the Klingons, while Saru is like “what the FUCK ARE YOU DOING”, but they hold her off just long enough for the captain to come back… and then ha ha turns out SHE WAS RIGHT, THE KLINGONS WERE SIGNALLING FOR OTHER KLINGON SHIPS TO COME IN. To be continued I guess.

Not a great start. Doesn’t introduce the characters very well, we barely know three of them, and the main character is intensely unlikable.

The Vulcan Hello - ENT 2.5/10

We get a flashback showing Burnham joining the ship seven years ago. She’s weird, kinda racist, and very unfriendly and unlikable. They sure don’t know how to handle Vulcans still, after all these years, huh?

Meanwhile, the Klingon dude on the other ship is trying to unify the Klingons to go to war with the Federation. At first they’re apprehensive, but he convinces them once the rest of the Federation arrive. BTW I hate the Klingons here? They are ONLY speaking Klingon, and I HATE how it sounds. It does NOT sound like Klingon, it sounds…. weird. This on top of the constant cuts and terrible editing. It’s exhausting to look at, AND listen to, this show!!

Okay this Klingon dude, T’Kuvma, believes the Federation is a blight on society and will destroy the Klingon Empire, because they will like… wear down individuality or some shit. It’s pretty much Eddington’s stupid arguments all over again.

The ship is almost destroyed. The bring is open to air, only held by a faltering force field. And then Burnham is contacted by her adopted dad, Sarek, through a fucking Force Diad. Anyways, shit is bad. The Klingons feign “okay let’s stop and cease fire :)” and then just blow up the Federation admiral’s ship anyway.

They’re basically dead in the water, but use the confusion of the battlefield to try a sneak attack instead. They see the Klingon leader tractoring in dead bodies so he can do final rites, and they booby trap them with bombs.

That is… SUCH a war crime? That is EXTREMELY A WAR CRIME.

They beam over to try and capture the Klingon leader. The captain is killed in the chaos, and then so is the Klingon leader. So uh, he’s gonna be a martyr. Burnham is sent home and charged and then we get this?

What the FUCK is this???? What is this Mass Effect Cerberus shit? This is Starfleet? Like, I know there’s the meme that Discovery is literally visibly dark, but JEEZE.

Anyways she’s charged and imprisoned for life. Starfleet has prisons now. Okay.

Context Is For Kings - ENT 3.5/10

Apparently the Admiral’s ship had a contingent of 8000 people. What the fuck?

It’s six months later, and Burnham is being transported to a prison. Everyone is like “THOSE PEOPLE DIED BECAUSE OF YOU.” Uh. She didn’t do anything? Like, the Klingons were going to do war regardless, she tried to mutiny to get them to fight first, and then failed. Her actions effected nothing at all.

The prison transport is almost broken but is found and saved by some ship called The Discovery. Everyone there treats them like bullshit. Hey, I love this nice happy future where everyone is better and treats people well and understand where they’re coming from, even if they’re prisoners. :)

Putting a lampshade on it doesn’t make it better, y’all.

Holy shit, the captain of the Discovery is like “Hey, you’re going to do labor here now. No questions, you can’t resist. Enjoy your life as a prisoner slave.” THE FUCKING HAPPY SOCIALIST FUTURE. She’s put in a room and given a roommate, someone who didn’t get the memo apparently. Her name is Sylvia Tilly, and she’s an awkward anxious mess. I’ve heard she’s one of the bright spots. Also some Weird Shit is happening as a “Black Alert” spreads through the ship.

We get LOTS of scenes of people being shitty assholes. No one is likable. Saru is the closest, he’s polite but logical. They force Burnham to do slave labor and don’t tell her anything. “I could do this easier if you told me what it is.” “Why would I tell a prisoner who is basically a temp the details of my research?” WHY WOULD YOU GIVE HER THIS JOB AT ALL?!?!

We meet the dude in charge of this science project. He’s kind of shitty and snooty. I don’t like him so far. He’s unhappy cause his research is being coopted for war, and he hates his captain, calls him a “warmonger”.

They’re sent to their sister ship, which blew up, and are trying to find what happened. There’s a LOT of meat. Well, I didn’t know this was a HORROR MOVIE. It is very weirdly gory and dark.

This is… not a Star Trek show.

It some weird horror monster thats eating and ripping into things. Burnham makes herself a distraction to save the others.

Back on the Discovery, after capturing the weird monster thing, the captain, Captain Lorca, is like “hey you should join us”. She goes “What? No.” he explains that they were using a space fungus that can produce magic propulsion. She decides to join them. Why? Okay, sure dude.

Okay, that was all a three parter opening movie basically to set up the ACTUAL show.

The Butcher’s Knife Cares Not for the Lamb’s Cry - ENT 3/10

What a title.

Burnham walks in onto the Bridge, now a member of the crew, into a simulation of a battle. They all die. Lorca then rants about how their new spore drive means they have to be prepared for war. Oh so we’re just talking about this now???

Apparently the Klingon ship has been sitting there for six months, unfixed. Also we find out that they ATE the captain?? That’s not what Klingons do. I refuse to accept that.

Anyways Lorca tells Burnham to study the monster from the last episode. Why he even has that, who knows. He wants Burnham to weaponize it. He also assigns his chief of safety, Landry, to study it with her. They turn the monster, which they nickname Ripper, into a fucking metaphor for Burnham. He’s biologically not designed to be a murderer. But he is anyway.

God I hate the writing in this show. Everyone is angry or grumpy 100% of the time. Except for Saru and Tilly, who are just anxious.

We also get a bunch of side scenes of the Klingon second in command of the dead Klingon guy. He’s raiding the old federation ship, floating dead in space.

A dilithium mine is under attack and Discovery is the only ship in the sector, take a drink. They try to use the sporedrive and it ends up almost killing them, cause it’s just not ready.

Hey look, the famous thing everyone makes fun of. No need to dwell on it further.

The lead engineer wants to pack up and go home, taking his spores with him. Lorca guilt trips him by playing a transmission from the mining colony over the loud speaker, for everyone on the ship to hear. You hear a girl crying to her dead mom telling her to wake up. This shit is almost LAUGHABLE with how over the top it is.

The head of security is like “okay we’re gonna study Ripper by shooting it and making it angry”. She does that and it of course kills her. What the FUCK was she thinking? The stupidest death I can think of in Star Trek. Burnham thinks it’s actually not an aggressive predator, it’s just defending itself. After a bit more testing, she determines that Ripper, a Tardigrade, is actually a symbiotic creature with the spores, and they manage to use it to jump and save the colony. By basically tortuing it. Yaaaay.

The episode ends with Burnham sad she betrayed Ripper, and then getting her captain’s last will and testament. It’s a hologram message where she’s like “Hi captain Burnham, I sure am glad you didn’t betray and get me killed :D Here have this~” and gives her an old telescope from her ready room… Did they abandon ship but take that telescope with them? What the fuck?

The vibes of this show are kinda rancid.

The Butcher’s Knife Cares Not for the Lamb’s Cry - ENT 3.5/10

Burnham is having nightmares about torturing Ripper. Meanwhile, Starfleet’s Admiral Council is like “We need to find more Tardigrades so we can make more spore drives”. They want to turn Starfleet into DUNE?

We are clearly using a self aware creature as a computer and navigator and tortuing it to do so. And only ONE person on the ship sees this as something Bad? Everyone is like “There’s no evidence things are bad”. The doctor LITERALLY SAID it was getting weaker??? Also you can just look at it.

Lorca is kidnapped by some Klingons and thrown on a prison ship. His roomie is one Harcourt Fenton Mudd. Yes, THAT Harry Mudd. He’s a common pest in TOS, and here he is. He is very attractive. My community made fun of me for thinking that. The Klingons regularly torture and beat people in the jail, just for fun. We also meat another prisoner, Ash Tyler. I never know who in this show will be important or who will just be killed. Mudd is also like “Starfleet is the reason this war happened. Life is shitty for us little folk.” Gee I sure am glad they keep hammering in how actually terrible the Star Trek world is.

Saru finds out that Burnham is still trying to get them to stop tortuing this poor animal and is so angry he confines her to quarters. What the FUCK is this show? Ripper shuts down after MORE torture and Saru is like “wake it up again. Why are we even talking about this?” WHY ARE YOU IN STARFLEET? WHY ARE YOU PEOPLE LIKE THIS?! He demands Stamets revive Ripper and get the ship flyable, which Stamets agrees to.

Lorca and Ash manage to escape, leaving and locking Mudd in the prison ship. Discovery rescues him and then flees… turns out Stamets basically turned himself into Ripper and used the torture machine on himself, nearly killing himself.

There’s a scene where Burnham tells Saru “you did well in the captain’s chair”. He did not. He tortured a fucking animal and nearly killed his engineer, who is the ONLY GUY WHO CAN GET THIS DRIVE WORKING. If he dies, they are all fucked.

They release Ripper into space and it flies away.

Oh hey, Stamets and the doctor are gay together. Which one of them do you think is gonna be killed so we can bury our gays? Well, Stamets is acting… strange after his Episode, so…

I hated this, but at LEAST it round aboutly said “yes, tortuing this sentient creature is bad”. Though no one got comeuppance for it, and they barely acknowledge it.

…I think I was right to fear Discovery.
